intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer calming, organic atmospheres that feel personal and supportive rather than clinical
facade.label: Soft, warm lighting
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm color temperatures, gentle gradients, and ambient glow to create a sanctuary feel for vulnerable health tracking
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer crisp, cool, clinical aesthetics that feel objective and scientifically neutral—or wants something entirely different on mood axis

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer calming, organic atmospheres that feel personal and supportive rather than clinical
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use warm color temperatures, gentle gradients, and ambient glow to create a sanctuary feel for vulnerable health tracking

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a conversational, lightweight tone over clinical language when logging health data
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard voice should feel approachable and encourage frequent micro-interactions rather than formal data entry. Messages like 'How's your energy today?' instead of 'Log vitals.'
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clarity and authority in health tracking—professional, measured language builds trust. Dashboard should sound like a healthcare tool, not a friend.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing health metrics change immediately (without animation) for quick scanning and trust
facade.axisTargeted: animated transitions vs instant state changes
facade.label: Instant number updates
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard favors speed and clarity over delight; metric readability is higher priority than motion feedback
facade.rejectImplies: Users want to *see* the change happening; motion adds confidence and reduces cognitive load of parsing new values

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments may prefer gentle, flowing color transitions that feel calming and non-clinical, reducing stress around data input.
facade.label: Soft gradients
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use smooth color transitions, warm-to-cool palettes, and avoid harsh contrasts. Implies a gentle, approachable aesthetic.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer stark, high-contrast interfaces or solid flat colors. Suggests the design should lean clinical, minimal, or bold rather than soothing.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments prefer seeing trends at a glance (micro-charts) over reading summary text for each metric
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data visualization vs narrative summary
facade.label: Time series sparklines
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into embedded micro-visualizations: small inline charts for each metric (weight, sleep, mood) showing the last 30-90 days at a glance. Minimal labels. Prioritize pattern recognition over exact numbers.
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward text-first summaries: use prose insights ('You've been sleeping better this week') and highlight key metrics in large typography rather than graphical trends. Numbers as prose anchors, not visual patterns.

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments may prefer clean, structured digital interfaces with high contrast and clear data visibility over warm organic atmospheres
facade.label: Bright, minimal grid
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use stark white backgrounds, monospace typography, and precisely-aligned data tables to prioritize information density and scientific clarity over sanctuary feel
facade.rejectImplies: Confirms that warmth and organic design (calming, personal, supportive) matters more than clinical precision and digital efficiency for this use case

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left sidebar for navigation with content displayed as individual cards in a main column, reducing cognitive load for multi-experiment tracking
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure and content grouping model
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values clear separation between navigation and content; likely prefers modular, scannable layouts; may want persistent context while exploring different experiments
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor tabbed navigation, full-width layouts, or list-based organizations; likely wants more screen space for content; may find sidebars visually heavy or restrictive
